About

I am a PhD candidate in Economics at University Name. My research lies at the intersection of labor economics and applied microeconomics, with a focus on how technological change shapes the structure of work, skill demand, and firm hiring behavior.

I use large-scale administrative records and online job posting data, combined with quasi-experimental methods, to study these questions. Before starting my PhD, I worked as a research assistant at Institution.

I am on the 20XX–XX academic job market. Please feel free to reach out at zhiwei.shang1@gmail.com.

News

  • Apr 2026 Presented job market paper at the NBER Summer Institute.
  • Mar 2026 New working paper on firm AI adoption is now online.
  • Jan 2026 Awarded the Award Name dissertation fellowship.

Last updated: April 2026.
